meta.note: Faithful, extractive summaries; every item links to its original source.

items.headline: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
items.summary: IIn March, Meta's Oversight Board called on the company to "meet its public commitments and employ its own tools" to help quell the spread of deceptive generative AI content across platforms. Meta responded in July by introducing Content…

items.headline: Utility companies promise to spare us from AI’s energy bill
items.summary: In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in the US are now promising to do something about it. The Wall Street Journal reports that…

items.headline: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
items.summary: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.
